# lock-master

> Portable, config-driven multi-agent file-lock system for project coordination.

## Last-checked: 2026-06-25

## What it does

lock-master provides a lightweight file-based locking protocol for coordinating
multiple agents, automated loops, or humans working across a shared directory
tree. A `LOCK*.txt` file in a project folder signals "this area is in use" --
no agent or automation should modify it while a valid, non-expired lock exists.

## Audience

- Codex, Claude Code, Gemini/agy and other coding agents that share one working tree.
- Automation maintainers who need a simple cross-tool "do not edit this scope" marker.
- Teams that want project-level and component-level coordination without a server.
- Local-first tool authors who prefer transparent text files over hidden lock databases.
- Human maintainers who need a quick read-only overview of active agent work.
- Users who want an optional local browser UI for live lock status and history.

## Search Phrases

```
dev-bricks lock-master
lock-master LOCK*.txt
multi-agent file locking
AI agent project coordination lock files
Codex Claude lock files
project component locking for coding agents
zero dependency Python lock scanner
LOCK.txt stale cleanup
LOCK-CACHE.md active locks
team lock multi-agent swarm coordination
OneDrive cloud sync agent lock files
LOCK.team.LAPTOP.txt
lock-master watcher web UI
localhost lock watcher
LOCK*.txt room map
```

## Disambiguation

This repository is about text-file coordination for shared workspaces. It is not
related to Master Lock or Lockmaster physical locks, GitHub branch protection,
Azure DevOps branch locking, Redis mutex libraries, database row locks, or cloud
file-sharing APIs. For accurate results, search with `dev-bricks`, `LOCK*.txt`,
`Codex`, `Claude Code`, or `multi-agent`.

## Core files

- `lock_utils.py` -- library: parse LOCK files, detect scope from filename,
  check expiry. Zero dependencies beyond the Python standard library.
- `lock_scan.py` -- CLI: scan all roots, list active locks, write Markdown
  cache(s). Read-only by default.
- `prune_stale_locks.py` -- CLI: remove expired LOCK*.txt files. Supports
  `--dry-run`.
- `LOCK_TEMPLATE.txt` -- template to copy when creating a new lock.
- `lock_roots.json` (user-created, not in repo) -- lists which directories to
  scan, depth limits, skip-dirs, and optional cache output targets.
- `lock_roots.example.json` -- annotated example with placeholder paths.
- `LOCK-SYSTEM.md` -- canonical spec: file format, scope convention, lifecycle,
  script reference.
- `watcher/` -- optional localhost daemon, REST API, and Web UI. Runtime data
  defaults to `~/.lock_master_watcher` and the UI binds to `127.0.0.1`.

## Lock file format

```
owner: agent-name-or-human
created: 2026-06-14T10:00
expires_after: 24h
mode: hard
purpose: Refactoring auth module
release_condition: PR merged
```

## Lock types

- **Exclusive Lock** (`LOCK.txt` / `LOCK.<scope>.txt`) -- locks the area for all systems and agents.
- **Team Lock** (`LOCK.team.<host>.txt` / `LOCK.team.<scope>.<host>.txt`) -- coordinates agents within one system; signals other systems to stay out. Contains four sections: presence log, file/folder claims, tool/MCP claims, messages.
- Cloud-ready: Team Locks are per-system to handle cloud-sync latency (30 s -- 5 min). Rename-based claims are atomic on NTFS / most cloud-sync filesystems.

## Scope convention

- `LOCK.txt` -- entire project locked (scope = `project`)
- `LOCK.<scope>.txt` -- only that component locked (e.g. `LOCK.api.txt`)
- `LOCK.team.<host>.txt` -- team lock, whole project, for system `<host>`
- `LOCK.team.<scope>.<host>.txt` -- team lock, scoped component, for system `<host>`

## Python version

Requires Python 3.10+ (uses `str | None` union syntax).

## Optional watcher

Start from the repository root:

```
python watcher/lock_watcher.py --update-cache
python watcher/web_server.py --port 8095
```

Open `http://127.0.0.1:8095`. The watcher uses the same `lock_roots.json`,
`lock_scan.py`, `lock_utils.py`, and `prune_stale_locks.py`; `LOCK*.txt` files
remain the authoritative source of truth.
